description_long: This object is a compact, modern shotgun with a matte black metal body and a wooden grip panel on the handle. The frame is angular and robust, with a rectangular profile and a squared-off trigger guard. The wooden grip features visible grain and is fastened with two dark screws, providing a classic contrast to the otherwise industrial design. The barrel is short and thick, with a single bore visible from the front, and the top of the receiver has simple, low-profile sights. The trigger is housed within a squared guard, and there are no visible external mechanisms such as a pump or lever. The overall design suggests a utilitarian, concealable weapon suitable for close-quarters use in urban environments.
description: A compact, black metal shotgun with a wooden grip and squared trigger guard, featuring a single short barrel and minimal external mechanisms.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, the wooden grip panel with two screws, and the squared trigger guard.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the narrow profile of the shotgun and the alignment of the grip and barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the wooden grip panel and the angular, boxy frame.
description_view_3: Front view showing the single barrel opening and the squared-off muzzle.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, short barrel
attribute_trigger_guard_design: squared, medium-sized, with straight edges and no finger grooves
